A

add(T) - Method in class MyClassicPriorityQueue
Inserts t into the heap and records its starting index in the map before bubbling up to the correct position.
add(T) - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Inserts t at the end of the heap then bubbles it up to restore the heap property.

B

bubbleUp(int) - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Moves the element at the given index upward until its parent is no larger than it, restoring the heap property above the index.

C

compareTo(WeightedElement<E, W>) - Method in class WeightedElement
Compares this WeightedElement to another by weight only.

D

decreaseKey(T, T) - Method in class MyClassicPriorityQueue
Decreases the value of target to smallerValue and returns the old value.

E

equals(Object) - Method in class WeightedElement
Two WeightedElements are equal when both their element and weight are equal.

G

getElement() - Method in class WeightedElement
Returns the element stored in this WeightedElement.
getIndexMap() - Method in class MyClassicPriorityQueue
Returns the internal index map for unit testing purposes.
getWeight() - Method in class WeightedElement
Returns the weight stored in this WeightedElement.

H

hashCode() - Method in class WeightedElement
Hash code is derived from both element and weight to be consistent with equals.
heap - Variable in class MyPriorityQueue
 

I

isEmpty() - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Returns true if the heap contains no elements.
isHeap() - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Iteratively verifies the heap ordering property for every element by checking that each node is no larger than its left and right children.

M

MyClassicPriorityQueue<T> - Class in Unnamed Package
A min-heap priority queue that extends MyPriorityQueue by maintaining a private HashMap from each element to its current index in the heap.
MyClassicPriorityQueue() - Constructor for class MyClassicPriorityQueue
Constructs an empty classic priority queue.
MyPriorityQueue<T> - Class in Unnamed Package
A generic min-heap priority queue backed by an ArrayList.
MyPriorityQueue() - Constructor for class MyPriorityQueue
Constructs an empty priority queue.

P

peek() - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Returns the minimal element without removing it from the heap.
poll() - Method in class MyClassicPriorityQueue
Removes and returns the minimal element.
poll() - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Removes and returns the minimal element (the root of the heap).
PriorityQueueTest - Class in Unnamed Package
Unit tests for WeightedElement, MyPriorityQueue and MyClassicPriorityQueue.
PriorityQueueTest() - Constructor for class PriorityQueueTest
 

S

siftDown(int) - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Moves the element at the given index downward until both of its children are no smaller than it, restoring the heap property below the index.
size() - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Returns the number of elements currently in the heap.
swap(int, int) - Method in class MyClassicPriorityQueue
Swaps the elements at positions i and j and updates both of their map entries so the map always reflects the current heap layout.
swap(int, int) - Method in class MyPriorityQueue
Swaps the elements at positions i and j in the heap.

T

testAddReturnValueAndIsHeap() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
add must return true and the heap property must hold after every insertion.
testClassicDecreaseKeyWeightedIntInt() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey should work correctly for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer>.
testClassicDecreaseKeyWeightedStringStringRejected() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey on WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String> with a larger weight must return null.
testClassicPQEmptyBehavior() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
An empty classic queue should return null and have an empty map.
testClassicPQMapIndicesConsistentAfterAdd() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Every element in the heap should have its index correctly recorded in the map.
testClassicPQMapIndicesConsistentAfterPoll() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
The map should remain consistent after a poll operation
testClassicPQMatchesJavaPQ() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Poll order of MyClassicPriorityQueue must match java.util.PriorityQueue.
testClassicPQPollOrder() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
poll should return elements in ascending order.
testClassicPQSingleElement() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
A single-element classic queue should add the element to the map at index 0.
testClassicWeightedIntIntMatchesJavaPQ() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Poll order for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer> in classic queue must match oracle.
testClassicWeightedStringStringMatchesJavaPQ() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Poll order for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String> in classic queue must match oracle.
testDecreaseKeyEqualValueReturnsNull() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey should return null when smallerValue equals target.
testDecreaseKeyLargerValueReturnsNull() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey should return null without any change when smallerValue is larger.
testDecreaseKeyLeafElement() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey on a leaf element should restore heap property and update poll order.
testDecreaseKeyMapUpdatedCorrectly() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
After decreaseKey the old key must be removed from the map and the new key added.
testDecreaseKeyMiddleElement() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey on a mid-heap element should bubble it up correctly.
testDecreaseKeyNotInQueueReturnsNull() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey should return null when target is not present in the queue.
testDecreaseKeyRootElement() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
decreaseKey on the current root element should still return the correct old value.
testDuplicateValues() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Duplicate values must be handled correctly and match the oracle.
testEmptyQueueBehavior() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
An empty queue should return null for peek and poll and report isHeap true.
testMatchesJavaPQIntegers() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
poll order and peek must match java.util.PriorityQueue for a general Integer sequence.
testPeekDoesNotRemove() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
peek should not change the size or the next poll result.
testSingleElement() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
A queue with one element should behave correctly for all operations.
testWeightedElementCompareToIntInt() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
compareTo for Integer weight: ordering should match Integer.compareTo.
testWeightedElementCompareToStringString() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
compareTo for String weight: ordering should match String.compareTo.
testWeightedElementEquals() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
equals should be based on both element and weight fields.
testWeightedElementGetters() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Getters should return the exact values passed to the constructor.
testWeightedElementHashCode() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
hashCode must be consistent with equals for HashMap usage.
testWeightedIntIntEqualWeights() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Equal weights with different elements: all elements should still drain correctly.
testWeightedIntIntMatchesJavaPQ() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Poll order by weight must match the oracle for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'Integer,Integer>.
testWeightedStringStringMatchesJavaPQ() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Poll order by String weight must match the oracle for WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String>.
testWeightedStringStringSingleElement() - Method in class PriorityQueueTest
Single-element WeightedElementinvalid input: '<'String,String> edge case.
toString() - Method in class WeightedElement
Returns a human-readable string in the form (element, weight).

W

WeightedElement<E,W> - Class in Unnamed Package
A generic container pairing an element of type E with a weight of type W.
WeightedElement(E, W) - Constructor for class WeightedElement
Constructs a WeightedElement with the given element and weight.
